title: PDate for recall-based dates (partial OK), Date for definite events (full date required)
description: OpenClinica distinguishes two date types: Date (accepts only DD-MMM-YYYY full dates) and PDate (accepts DD-MMM-YYYY, MMM-YYYY, or YYYY partial dates). OC documentation warns: requiring a full date when only month/year is known is 'a major hazard for analysis' — users pick '1st' or '15th' placeholders that look authoritative but are not. DEFAULT CATEGORIZATION: Date for events the site staff records contemporaneously and knows in full (informed consent date, randomization date, visit date, sample collection date, dose admin date, enrollment date). PDate for recall-based or self-reported dates (CMSTDAT, CMENDAT, AESTDAT, AEENDAT, MHSTDAT, prior procedure dates, date of diagnosis, date of birth when partial-DOB is acceptable). CAVEATS: OC Rules don't support PDate operators in rule expressions; cross-form calculations involving PDate fields need flagging (status=FLAGGED) with a brief description noting the limitation. The existing manual partial-date decomposition pattern in references/xlsform-patterns.md ([prefix]DAT_YEAR / _MON / _DAY / _UNK with calculate fields) is preserved as-is — whether to migrate to native PDate is a separate decision tracked in Open Items.
rationale: Forcing a full date when the subject only remembers the month produces fictional precision: the coordinator picks day 1 or day 15 as a placeholder, and downstream analyses treat that placeholder as a real date. PDate handles the partial-recall case honestly by accepting MMM-YYYY or YYYY-only entries. The categorization distinguishes 'contemporaneous and knowable in full' events (clinic visits, sample collection, consent — the site has the actual date) from 'recall-based' events (medical history, prior medications, adverse event onsets — the subject is reporting from memory).

flag.message: Field ${field.name} (type=${field.type}) — verify Date-vs-PDate choice matches the data being captured. Date: contemporaneous events the site knows in full (RFICDAT, visit date, randomization date, sample collection, dose admin). PDate: recall-based events (CMSTDAT/CMENDAT, AESTDAT/AEENDAT, MHSTDAT, prior procedures, diagnosis date, partial-DOB).

effect.soft: When generating date fields, choose Date for contemporaneous events and PDate for recall-based events per the categorization in the rule's description. For PDate fields used in cross-form calculations, add a FLAGGED status with a note: OC Rules don't support PDate operators in rule expressions. The categorization requires judgment about each field's clinical context — handled here in soft.

history.note: Imported from conventions.md §9 during Phase B.1d. Hybrid because Date-vs-PDate selection requires judging each field's clinical context (contemporaneous vs recall-based) — that judgment can be partially automated by field-name pattern matching (*STDAT/*ENDAT are usually recall-based on medical history forms but contemporaneous on visit forms) but the heuristic is unreliable enough to keep the rule advisory in effect. The Open Items reference is the question of whether to migrate from the [prefix]DAT_YEAR/_MON/_DAY decomposition pattern to native PDate — that decision is tracked in the source conventions.md Open Items section and not resolved by this convention.
